member.office_address: 570 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3225

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
As a member of the House Appropriations Committee, Congressman Morelle is pleased to accept appropriations requests from interested parties. Priority will be given to requests from/on behalf of our constituents, so we encourage applicants to clearly detail benefits to NY25 in the submission form.

In addition to submitting this form, we encourage you to connect with our policy team to discuss your request and share any additional support materials. If you need assistance finding the correct staff contact, please call our DC office (202-225-3615). We will do our best to accommodate all meeting requests, but priority will be given to our NY25 constituents--thank you for understanding.

If you need to make edits to your request once it has been submitted, please reach out to our office.

This form is NOT for Community Project Funding Requests.

sections.description: General organization and point of contact details.
General details regarding the subcommittee, agency, and account for the request.

fields.label: Subcommittee
fields.help_text: Select the relevant House Appropriations subcommittee.
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the Commerce, Justice, Science and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: As specified in the provided reference values.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: Standard account name as per reference values for SMD funding.

fields.label: Program/Language Title
fields.rationale: Title for the programmatic funding request identified in the context.

fields.label: Proposal Description (limit 500 characters)
fields.draft_value:
* Requesting $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration and discovery.
* Supports essential missions across Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences.
* Ensures funding stability for decadal survey priorities and critical workforce capabilities following the FY2026 budget disruptions.
* Strengthens the STEM pipeline and innovation economy in NY-25.
fields.rationale: Drafted based on the $9 billion funding ask for SMD and core advocacy talking points regarding U.S. leadership and the STEM pipeline.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for the Science Mission Directorate, which is a programmatic request.

sections.description: Details regarding the requested funding levels and historical context.

fields.label: Funding action
options: Support total funding level of (specify amount below)
Funding should be increased by (specify amount below)
Funding should be reduced by (specify amount below)
fields.draft_value: Support total funding level of (specify amount below)
fields.rationale: The advocacy goal is to support a specific topline funding level of $9,000,000,000 for NASA Science.

fields.label: Specify funding Level (if required)
fields.help_text: Enter the specific dollar amount requested for FY27.
fields.rationale: This is the exact funding ask amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Was this request included in the FY26 President's Budget Request (PBR)?
fields.rationale: NASA Science is an existing program and was included in the FY26 President's Budget Request, though at a significantly lower level ($3.908 billion) than the enacted amount.

fields.label: If included in FY26 PBR, what was the amount requested?
fields.rationale: The FY26 President's Budget Request amount from the authoritative SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: FY26 Funding Level in House/Senate bills
fields.rationale: The FY26 Enacted amount reflects the final consensus level in the legislative process for that year.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Funding Level
fields.rationale: The authoritative FY26 Enacted amount provided in the reference data.

sections.description: Details for requests involving specific report or statutory language.

fields.label: Is this Bill or Report language?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, not a request for specific bill or report language.

fields.label: Proposed language (must be less than 1000 characters)
fields.rationale: The request context specifies that no bill or report language is requested for this programmatic funding request.

sections.description: Information detailing the local impact and priority of the request.

fields.label: NY25 Connections
fields.help_text: Describe how this request benefits New York's 25th District.
fields.draft_value:
* L3Harris Technologies in Rochester is executing a $376 million contract to develop primary optical hardware for the Wide Field Infrared Survey Telescope (Roman Space Telescope).
* Rochester Institute of Technology (RIT) received over $9.4 million in grants (FY23-25) for high-impact research, including CIBER-2 and next-generation Landsat simulations.
* The University of Rochester was awarded $1.28 million in NASA grants for scientific research during the same period.
* Total NASA Science obligations in NY-25 exceeded $97 million from FY2023 to FY2025.
fields.rationale: Cited specific local spending data for L3Harris, RIT, and University of Rochester found in the NASA spending context for NY-25.

fields.label: Local Value
fields.draft_value:
* Sustains high-tech manufacturing jobs at L3Harris and research positions at RIT and the University of Rochester.
* Advances critical optical technologies and sensor testing in the district that have direct commercial and defense spinoff applications.
* Inspires the next generation of NY-25 STEM professionals through active participation in cutting-edge missions like the Roman Space Telescope.
* Every dollar invested in NASA returns approximately three dollars to the economy, directly benefiting the Rochester regional innovation ecosystem.
fields.rationale: Connected local recipient activities (L3Harris optics, RIT sensors) to broader economic and workforce advocacy talking points.

fields.label: Priority Ranking (if submitting multiple requests)
fields.help_text: Indicate the priority of this request compared to others from your organization.
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific organizational priority question that cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Will this request be submitted by multiple Members of Congress?
fields.rationale: There is an existing multi-member letter led by Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu supporting NASA Science funding.

top_contracts.description: Optical Telescope Assembly (OTA) this contract provides for all efforts and responsibilities for the personnel, services, materials, equipment, and facilities necessary to build, refurbish, or modify the ota as required to meet the wide field infrared survey Telescope (WFIRST) performance requirements.
